summary:
Serum ceruloplasmin <20 mg/dL and elevated >40 µg Cu/24 hours urine indicate Wilson Disease; these abnormal values reflect impaired copper metabolism characteristic of this condition. Answer choice D accurately reflects a diagnostic criterion based on altered copper handling seen clinically in patients diagnosed with Wilson’s disease. Other options misrepresent typical laboratory results associated with the disorder. To summarize:

Wilson disease diagnosis involves low serum ceruloplasmin (<20 mg/dL) and increased urinary copper (>40 μg/Cu/24 h), reflecting disrupted copper processing. Option D correctly represents an accurate test result consistent with Wilson disease pathophysiology.
